markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Add validation that UserManual entries are current with touched features, APIs, tools, and primitives. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualFreshnessCheck leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualFreshnessCheck is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Freshness documentation and spec seed now cover every runtime FreshnessVerdictKind, including non-page tool/feature/alias verdicts; RED/GREEN proof and adversarial review PASS.

evidence.summary: Seeded UserManual surface and UMSPEC-002 now name all runtime freshness verdict kinds: page, tool-entry, feature-entry, and legacy-alias states.

RED proved missing non-page verdict vocabulary; GREEN proves every FreshnessVerdictKind::as_str value appears in the seeded UserManual and UMSPEC-002 surfaces, followed by full suites and cargo check.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.
Hubble adversarial review returned PASS; no blocking findings, with one non-blocking future hardening idea to count read receipts on the missing-link path.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-204 requires the UserManualFreshnessCheck surface to detect and document drift between the stored UserManual, the compiled-in seed corpus, and the declared surface registry; the V2 fix adds non-page (tool/feature/legacy-alias) verdict vocabulary. The code delivers a real, PostgreSQL-backed freshness check wired to a live route, with all 12 typed verdict kinds documented in the seeded manual and UMSPEC-002 and enforced by tests including real-PG fixtures.
findings: freshness.rs:39-77 defines FreshnessVerdictKind with all 12 variants; every non-Current variant is is_problem()=true and maps to a real defect category (page content, tool/feature/alias drift, uncovered/dangling surface, unseeded version).

freshness.rs:98-337 check_freshness performs real store queries (list_pages, list_tool_entries, list_feature_entries, list_legacy_aliases, anchors_by_kind, get_version) plus surface-registry comparison; substantive, not stubbed.
api/user_manual.rs:62 and :439 expose live GET /usermanual/freshness calling the real check_freshness against PostgreSQL and appending a freshness_checked receipt; runtime-wired, not placeholder.

seed.rs:984-1004 (usermanual-surface page) and spec_seed.rs:55-66 (UMSPEC-002) both enumerate all 12 verdict strings including the V2 non-page verdicts; recovery guidance states resync covers 'changed pages, tool entries, feature entries, and legacy aliases'.

user_manual_content_tests.rs:617-670 mt204_freshness_docs_name_every_verdict_kind asserts every FreshnessVerdictKind::as_str appears in both the seeded page and UMSPEC-002, with a negative assertion that resync guidance is not page-only; this is the RED/GREEN proof closing the V1 gap.

Real-PG proof: user_manual_freshness_fixture_tests.rs:16-88 and content_tests.rs:678-711 call check_freshness against knowledge_pg() (Handshake-managed PostgreSQL) and assert StaleContent / MissingPage verdicts; satisfies sub-rule 2.
UMSPEC-002 target anchor '10.15.8 UserManual migration bridge' exists in the current v02.194 spec bundle (10-product-surfaces.md:8151); spec_seed rows point at live anchors.
No deferred-live escapes: grep of freshness.rs, spec_seed.rs, and api/user_manual.rs found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/pending-MT placeholders.
Aside (non-blocking, expected): MT contract has no implementation_commit field; work landed in commit fcf9bce3 (MT-193..208 group) plus checkpoint c49afb7f. Paperwork-only, not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
